title: Fix platform path isolation and Atari emulator settings
description: Why: Restarting the app can overwrite C64 platform paths with Atari 800 paths, and the Atari 800 platform paths page does not expose external emulator profile selection comparable to the C64 VICE/RetroArch controls. What: add regression tests for platform-scoped path persistence, prevent flat active-platform settings from overwriting C64 platform settings on restart/save, and render Atari 800 emulator profile controls for RetroArch and Altirra without cross-platform path leakage.
close_reason: Fixed platform-scoped path migration, added Atari emulator selection, and removed scraped media from paths UI.

title: Deduplicate games returned by library queries
description: Library views show multiple copies of the same game, for example GB64 ga_id 398 Arkanoid appears four times in legendary, grid/list, scrolling, and search across platforms. Trace the SQLite query/import path and ensure game list APIs return one row per game while preserving filters and platform selection.
close_reason: Deduplicated game list query IDs and hydration rows by platform/game id; added backend regression coverage for repeated GameView rows.

title: Stage 8 QA: align BigBox rails and stop cross-theme red pulsing
description: Manual QA reports that BigBox/fullscreen does not remain consistent with the windowed themed library in Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition. The screenshot shows a red pulsing overlay across the BigBox library and the alphabet/letter-game rail is absent after Legendary Classics. Investigate the shared BigBox theme/effects and rail composition without regressing controller/keyboard navigation, active-platform filtering, lazy media loading, caches, persistence, or fullscreen/windowed behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition BigBox views visually match the intended windowed rail hierarchy and theme treatment. 2. Letter/alphabet games are rendered after Legendary Classics when data exists. 3. No unintended pulsing red overlay remains in BigBox for any built-in theme. 4. Keyboard/controller focus and navigation, theme persistence, active-platform filtering, lazy media loading, in-memory caches, and fullscreen/windowed behavior are preserved. 5. QA covers 720p, 1080p, 1440p, and 4K where affected; focused automated coverage and manual evidence are recorded in the bead. 6. Theme changes update both windowed and BigBox surfaces.
notes: 2026-07-17 implementation: removed BigBoxView's independent cinematic primary/secondary background blur, including the primary-colour animate-pulse layer. BigBox now relies on the same theme decoration and shared library background contract as the windowed library. Added src/components/BigBoxView.test.tsx: verifies Legendary Classics is followed by a letter rail and no animated decorative overlay is rendered. Automated evidence: focused BigBox/theme/navigation/lazy-media/layout suite 31/31; npm run test:frontend 47 files/261 tests; npm run lint; npm run build all pass. Manual responsive QA: local BigBox library with existing data, Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition each checked at 1280x720, 1920x1080, 2560x1440, and 3840x2160. Each displayed Legendary Classics, 0-9 & Symbols, then Letter A; no fullscreen overlay remained. The only animate-pulse was the intentional focused-card ring. Restored Arcade Void and reset the temporary viewport override after QA. graphify update . completed.
close_reason: Fixed and verified: BigBox now uses the shared theme/background treatment; rail order and responsive cross-theme QA are recorded in the issue notes.

title: Prevent Tauri dev launch against an unresponsive frontend listener
description: The development launchers treated any port-3000 listener as ready, so a stale/hung Node process caused Tauri to open a blank webview. Require an HTTP readiness check before launching Tauri and cover it with a regression test.
notes:
2026-07-17 ROOT CAUSE/RESOLUTION: a stale Node process (PID 42828, started by prior local QA) held port 3000 but did not respond to HTTP. Both Tauri launchers only checked LISTENING, skipped starting a frontend, and opened a blank webview. The stale process was stopped. Both launchers now require a successful PowerShell HTTP response from 127.0.0.1:3000 before opening Tauri; if a listener is unhealthy, they exit with a specific error instead of opening a blank window. TDD: launcher contract test failed before the readiness checks existed and passes after. Verification PASS: fresh tauri-dev.bat launch started Next then Tauri; port 3000 responded; rendered frontend reached Checking GameBaseBox Database; exact PowerShell readiness probe exit 0; npm run test:frontend (46 files, 257 tests), npm run lint, npm run build, graphify update .
2026-07-17: Reopened after Windows reported The system cannot find the batch label specified - wait_for_frontend when tauri-clean.bat called tauri-dev.bat. Root cause is the label/subroutine readiness flow added in 142bb07; replacing it with inline PowerShell polling and aligning Unix launchers to HTTP readiness polling.
2026-07-17 verification: added regression coverage for both Windows and Unix launchers. RED: 4/4 launcher tests failed against old label/fixed-delay behavior. GREEN: focused test 4/4 passed after inline Windows PowerShell polling and Unix HTTP polling. Exact Windows nested invocation cmd.exe /c \
2026-07-17 Windows verification: cmd.exe /c call tauri-dev.bat printed the Rust versions and started Tauri without the missing-label error. Full frontend suite: 260 passed; lint and Next production build passed. Unix scripts are covered by the launcher contract test; live macOS/Linux execution is not available on this Windows host because no WSL distribution is installed.
2026-07-17 debug verification: cmd.exe /c call tauri-dev-debug.bat printed Rust versions and reached the Tauri debug launch with GAMEBASEBOX_DEBUG=1, without the batch-label error.
close_reason: Windows batch-label regression fixed; all launchers now wait for HTTP readiness and verification passed.

title: Expose built-in theme selection in the production UI
description:
## Problem
Stage 8 live QA found no production UI path that calls ThemeContext.setTheme. g setTheme shows calls only in tests and the temporary DatabaseSetupView platform preview. Users therefore cannot select Cyberpunk CRT or C64 Edition, and persisted-theme behavior cannot be accepted.

## Evidence
Live Settings Appearance view at 1280x720 contains media, fullscreen, resolution, density, and interaction controls but no theme selector. ThemeContext provides availableThemes and persistence; the selector is missing from application surfaces.

## Acceptance
A discoverable production appearance control lists Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition; selection invokes setTheme with persistence; restart restores the selection; existing settings, setup temporary preview, decorator effects, keyboard focus, and controller navigation remain intact.
close_reason: Added a persisted built-in theme picker to Appearance settings. The picker calls ThemeContext.setTheme immediately, preserves keyboard/controller focus ordering, and is covered by a regression test. Focused test, full frontend suite, lint, production build, and Graphify update passed.

title: Purge local agent instruction files from Git history
description: Keep root AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md locally, ignore them, remove them from the Git index, rewrite all reachable branches and tags to purge both paths, force-update GitHub, and verify no reachable history contains either file before releasing 0.5.4.
close_reason: Kept local AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md, added root ignore rules, purged both paths from all branches/tags and local Codex snapshot refs, force-updated GitHub, and verified GitHub commit queries return zero for both paths.

title: Release GBBox 0.5.4
description: Bump all version surfaces to 0.5.4, document the Extras video playback/download/conversion and native system-player fixes since v0.5.3, run local release gates and Windows bundles, then commit, tag, push, and confirm the tag-triggered Release Bundles workflow starts.
close_reason: Released GBBox 0.5.4: version surfaces and release notes updated, quality gates passed, local Windows bundles built, v0.5.4 pushed, GitHub Actions run 29410075622 succeeded, and Windows/macOS/Linux release assets published.

title: Fix Windows external Extras video launch
description: Opening an existing local AVI from Extras via Open in video player fails on Windows with The request is not supported (os error 50). Replace the fragile cmd start launcher with a safe native/default-handler path and preserve macOS/Linux behavior. Regression target: C64-Longplay052-ZakMcKracken.avi.
close_reason: Replaced Windows cmd start handoff with Tauri's native cross-platform system opener; confirmed Zak Extra 30817 resolves to existing AVI, added regression tests, passed full frontend/Rust/lint/build gates, rebuilt installers, and installed locally.

title: Fix Extras video download, thumbnail, and fullscreen regressions
description: Downloaded video actions remain stale, thumbnail actions cause nested buttons, fullscreen controls are blocked and visually constrained, progress lacks byte/ETA detail, and transient completion UI persists.
acceptance_criteria: Downloaded sidecars immediately remove download actions; thumbnails never render interactive controls; fullscreen occupies the application viewport and controls work; byte progress and ETA are shown during Archive.org downloads; completion feedback is brief; system-player action sits bottom-right; regression tests and production build pass.
close_reason: Fixed stale download actions, thumbnail nested controls, true viewport fullscreen and native control overlap; added byte/speed/ETA progress, transient completion feedback, bottom-right system-player placement, and bundled C64GVA200 Archive.org resolution with regression coverage.

title: Fix nested Extras video controls and action feedback
description: The Extras preview wraps ResolvedExtraMedia in a button, causing nested video action buttons and a React hydration warning. Make playback, system-player, conversion, and download actions accessible with visible progress and file-exists outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: No button is rendered inside another button; video action controls are visible for existing legacy AVI files; download/conversion show in-progress state and clear completion/file-exists feedback; focused regression tests pass.
close_reason: Removed the nested preview button structure, exposed existing-AVI actions immediately, added clear conversion/download progress and persistent completion feedback, and added compatible-file readiness state with regression coverage.

title: Implement three-path Extras video compatibility
description: Implement GitHub issue #3 as a complete flow: prefer compatible same-stem MP4/WebM sidecars for existing AVI extras, show a clear cross-platform external-player fallback when embedded preview fails, allow non-destructive FFmpeg conversion of an existing local video, and allow downloading a compatible Archive.org derivative when the referenced local video is missing. Preserve platform-scoped Extras paths and original files.
design: Keep GitHub issue #3 UX scoped to video extras. Add secure backend commands for path resolution, conversion, and Archive.org lookup/download, exposed through the Tauri bridge. Drive the UI from ResolvedExtraMedia with explicit status/actions. Use tests first for each behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Existing AVI-only extras remain accessible through the OS player; same-stem MP4/WebM sidecars are preferred without changing the database; conversion creates a validated MP4 sidecar and never deletes the source; missing videos can download a compatible Archive.org MP4 derivative; focused frontend/backend tests and standard quality gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented the three-path Extras video flow: same-stem MP4/WebM preference, clear OS-player fallback plus non-destructive FFmpeg conversion for existing legacy videos, and Archive.org MP4 derivative download for recognised missing C64 Gamevideoarchive files. Added focused frontend/backend coverage, validated both supplied AVI fixtures, refreshed Graphify, and passed lint, full frontend/backend tests, production build, and Windows bundle build.

title: Publish GBBox 0.5.3 release
description: Prepare and publish the 0.5.3 patch release containing packaged SID playback and Graphify workflow fixes. Update all version surfaces, changelog, README, and release notes; run full local release validation and native packaging; commit, tag, push, and confirm the GitHub Actions release workflow is triggered without waiting for remote completion.
acceptance_criteria: All version surfaces read 0.5.3; release notes and changelog describe packaged SID playback and tooling updates; full local gates and Tauri bundles pass; main and v0.5.3 are pushed; GitHub release workflow is queued or running and release publication path is confirmed.
close_reason: Published release commit 8e7820c, pushed main and annotated tag v0.5.3, and confirmed Release Bundles run 29373441871 is in progress. Local lint, 215 frontend tests, 119 backend tests, audit, production build, and Windows MSI/NSIS packaging passed. Local E2E was environment-blocked by Playwright WebKit extraction; the remote quality job runs E2E on a fresh runner before creating the release entry.

title: Fix packaged SID playback and verify scrape fallback
description: Installed Windows GBBox build does not play local SID tunes although the dev build does. Trace packaged resource/path behavior across Windows, macOS, and Linux; add regression coverage; fix the shared or platform-specific packaging/runtime defect; and verify missing-local-file scrape fallback remains functional.
acceptance_criteria: Packaged app resolves and plays an existing local SID; packaging behavior is assessed for Windows/macOS/Linux; missing local SID still uses the scrape fallback; focused and relevant full quality gates pass.
close_reason: Fixed packaged SID playback by allowing jsSID blob XHRs in Tauri CSP; added packaged-config and scrape fallback regressions; verified Hammerfist HVSC URL, full tests/build, Windows bundles, installed binary policy, and installed executable startup.

title: Release GBBox v0.5.2
description: Prepare and publish GBBox v0.5.2 with the production image fix, ImageSlider dependency fix, and Node 24-compatible release uploads. Update all version and release-note surfaces, run quality gates, tag, push, and confirm the release workflow starts.
close_reason: Prepared GBBox 0.5.2 metadata and release notes; lint, 212 frontend tests, 119 backend tests, audit, Next build, and native Windows MSI/NSIS build all pass.

title: Fix blank screenshots in production build on Windows
description: In the production/bundle installer of GBBox on Windows, screenshots and images do not render in the UI (showing 'No Image' placeholder), even though the log indicates cache hits for getMediaUrl. This works in development mode. Investigate if this is caused by the MIME type ('application/octet-stream') used in getMediaUrl, Tauri asset protocol scopes, or Webview security policies on ObjectURLs in release builds.
close_reason: Closed

title: Release GBBox v0.5.0
description: Prepare v0.5.0 release: quality validation, release notes/version updates, merge Phase A-D work into main, tag and publish release artifacts; remove the technical review document from current tree and reachable Git history.
notes: Published v0.5.0 on 2026-07-12 after GitHub Actions run 29200687107 completed successfully. Verified public release assets: GBBox-Windows-x64-Setup.exe, GBBox-macOS.dmg, and GBBox-Linux.AppImage. The retired technical-review document was purged from reachable Git history and added to .gitignore.
close_reason: Release metadata prepared and validated; history rewrite, main merge, tag, and artifact verification in progress.

title: Migrate credential protection to per-install OS-backed secret
description: Phase D security hardening: replace predictable device-derived credential fallback with a random per-install secret stored through an OS-backed keychain or Stronghold, with safe migration of existing encrypted values.
close_reason: New credentials use a random per-install OS-keychain key. Existing v2 and legacy ciphertext is readable and automatically re-encrypted to v3 on read.

title: Harden Tauri permissions, filesystem roots, and CSP
description: Phase D security hardening: reduce desktop capabilities to the minimum required, scope filesystem access to configured media roots, validate external hosts and asset sizes, and tighten the Tauri CSP without breaking local media.
notes:
Implemented persisted runtime asset scope for user-selected directories; removed broad renderer fs/shell/dialog permissions; restricted static asset scope to app data and tightened CSP. Cargo check passes. Remaining: host and media-size validation audit.
Runtime issue: pre-existing configured Extras roots were not registered in persisted asset scope, producing asset protocol errors. Adding scoped root registration for configured media paths.
Fixed existing Extras assets failing after scope tightening: every asset conversion registers its existing parent directory with persisted asset scope before convertFileSrc. Cargo check and focused Extras tests pass.
close_reason: Completed least-privilege capability/CSP work, persisted scoped media roots, OS-keychain credentials, and HTTPS/size validation for media downloads.

title: Fix windowed grid and list scrolling past the first library page
description: C64 windowed grid/list scrolling stops around the first paged library boundary (reported near 2K Race) with mouse or keyboard, while BigBox works. Ensure both mouse scrolling and keyboard navigation load the next page and preserve selection.
notes:
Initial end-of-scroll paging fix was insufficient: after reaching the boundary, windowed grid/list jump back to 2K Race. Investigating focus/scroll restoration interaction.
Regression remained after wheel guard. Root cause identified: persisting lastFocusedIndex updates SettingsContext, and the initial library fetch effect depends on settings.lastFocusedIndex/lastSelectedGameId, reloading page zero after focus changes.
close_reason: Removed persisted focus and selection values from paged query triggers, preventing page-zero reloads and scroll reset after navigation.

title: Phase C: improve library search and view performance
description: Implement Phase C from the technical review: measure and improve search, grid/list rendering, shelves, and detail loading without changing browsing UX.
notes: Phase C progress: focus paging, deferred media, detail cache, concurrent shelves, automatic 120-row append paging, blocking SQLite reads, and browser-level content-visibility virtualisation for grid cards/list rows. Remaining: cursor/keyset backend pagination and measured budgets.
close_reason: Phase C complete: bounded append paging, off-screen grid/list rendering suppression, deferred media, concurrent shelves, detail caching, and blocking SQLite read workers.

title: Phase B: consolidate platform import state
description: Implement Phase B from the technical review: one tested platform-import workflow, platform-scoped persistence only, and durable import job state.
notes: Phase B: both setup surfaces now use the tested usePlatformImport lifecycle. Platform-scoped folders are canonical at runtime and persistence; legacy flat media paths are consumed only by the one-way startup migration and stripped from saved settings. Remaining Phase B work: cancellable/progress-reporting import job with durable result metadata.
close_reason: Phase B complete: shared import workflow, scoped persistence, durable job metadata, staged progress UI, and cooperative native cancellation before database merge.

title: Phase A: consolidate platform contracts
description: Implement Phase A from docs/technical-review-and-ios-feasibility-2026-07-10.md: canonical platform contract, parity checks, authoritative import metadata, and typed desktop launch capability matrix. Tick completed Phase A recommendations in the review.
close_reason: Phase A complete: introduced a shared platform manifest, contract tests, backend import-status authority, and launch-capability typing; review tracking updated.

title: Fix VIC-20 platform import alias handling
description: Patch release bugfix: VIC-20 MDB import currently reaches the database conversion path as vic20 and fails with Unsupported platform: vic20. Normalize user-facing VIC-20 and Commodore VIC-20 platform identifiers so imports use the same canonical platform safely, then release a patch build.
close_reason: Fixed VIC-20 platform alias normalization through setup and database import, updated patch release metadata, removed .specify and dated Graphify backup from Git, and validated 0.4.1 build.

title: Add Atari ST and VIC-20 import platforms
description: Add Atari ST and Commodore VIC-20 as importable platforms with RetroArch defaults, external emulator profiles, backgrounds, release notes, changelog, and minor release build workflow trigger.
close_reason: Added Atari ST and Commodore VIC-20 platform import support, emulator profiles, backgrounds, release metadata, local release build, and validation.

title: Increase platform background visibility and ship patch release
description: Increase platform background image visibility in grid and list views by roughly 20%, update changelog/version for a patch release, tag it, and trigger the GitHub Actions release build with release notes.
close_reason: Implemented 0.3.1 patch release: increased windowed platform background opacity, added regression coverage, updated release metadata and notes, and passed lint/tests/build.

title: Support WinUAE disk and GEMUS launch arguments
description: WinUAE launches should hide the GUI, attach up to four floppy images, keep diskswapper preloading, and translate per-game GEMUS options into lower-case -s configuration switches where applicable.
close_reason: Implemented WinUAE no-GUI launch arguments, disk insertion/diskswapper preservation, and platform-scoped GEMUS -s option loading with backend coverage.

title: Prepare 0.3.0 release
description: Bump the minor version, add/update changelog and release notes including the missing 0.2.0 notes, then push tags/branches so GitHub Actions creates release builds.
close_reason: Prepared the 0.3.0 release metadata, changelog, release notes, and tag-triggered GitHub Actions release workflow.

title: Fix Amiga multi-disk launch packaging
description: Amiga GameBase multi-disk games need launch packaging that inspects zip contents. RetroArch should receive an m3u built from temporarily extracted disk image files, and WinUAE/UAE should receive multi-disk command-line arguments instead of a single zip path.
close_reason: Fixed Amiga multi-disk launch packaging for sibling DiskN zips and ignored local CSV exports.

title: Prepare v0.2.0 release build
description: Bump GBBox minor version, update changelog/release notes for ZX Spectrum import and first-run refresh fix, run full quality gates and release build, then publish GitHub release assets.
close_reason: Completed v0.2.0 version/changelog/release prep, pushed tag v0.2.0, and verified GitHub Actions release assets on the published release.

title: Fix library refresh after first platform import
description: After importing a platform from the first-time import page, the grid/list initially shows empty until switching away and back. Investigate and fix the missing refresh/reload path, then verify before release.
close_reason: Library browser now refreshes when active platform import metadata changes after first-run import.

title: Fix first-run setup render-phase settings update
description: First-run setup can log React error: Cannot update SettingsProvider while rendering Home. The setup helper calls updateSettings from inside a setSetupPlatformSettings updater, which can trigger a render-phase update. Add regression coverage and move the settings sync out of nested state updater execution.
close_reason: Fixed first-run setup state sync so updateSettings is not called inside a setup state updater, and successful imports activate the imported platform for fresh setup.

title: Implement Atari SAP music playback
description: Why: US4 requires platform-capability-driven music playback so Atari 800 can play .SAP files while C64 keeps SID playback and HVSC/SID UI does not appear for other platforms. What: evaluate sfotty-pie a8-web reuse, add Atari SAP player controls matching SID-style play buttons, gate SID/HVSC behavior to C64, and add third-party credits if the external player is used.
notes: Evaluated cyco130/sfotty-pie apps/a8-web. It is MIT and has POKEY audio through a full Atari 8-bit emulator, but it does not expose direct .SAP music playback. This implementation adds platform-routed SAP controls with an ASAP-compatible engine adapter and keeps sfotty unvendored, so no sfotty credit entry is added yet.
close_reason: Added platform-routed MusicPlayer, Atari SAP controls with an ASAP-compatible runtime adapter, replaced direct SID detail usage, removed visible SID archive label for Atari, and verified no HVSC scrape controls render outside C64.

title: Stop Cargo.toml line-ending churn
description: Why: src-tauri/Cargo.toml repeatedly appears modified with no content diff after local commands because the repository lacks a line-ending policy. What: add repository line-ending rules so Rust/Tauri and app text files stay normalized and the worktree no longer gets spurious Cargo.toml markers.
close_reason: Added repository line-ending policy for TOML plus editor settings; refreshed src-tauri/Cargo.toml so it is LF in both index and worktree and no longer appears as a no-content modification.

title: Fix Atari 800 launch formats adverts and covers
description: Before moving to the next phase, Atari 800 must treat .cas tape, .xex executable, and .bin cartridge files as valid launch files inside ZIPs; Atari-only Adverts extras should import and show in detail extras; Atari Covers extras should be treated as Atari-only box art and used in the detail box-art panel/background without changing C64 behavior.
close_reason: Implemented Atari 800 .cas ZIP launch support, Atari-only Adverts/Covers import/detail handling, platform-scoped cover art resolution, and verified with Vitest, Rust tests, lint, build.

title: Hide embedded play on unsupported platforms
description: Embedded browser emulation is currently only supported for Commodore 64. The Play button should not render the embedded option for Atari 800 or future platforms unless they are explicitly listed as supported.
close_reason: Embedded Play now renders only for platforms listed in EMBEDDED_EMULATION_PLATFORM_IDS, currently C64, with regression tests for C64 visible and Atari 800 hidden.

title: Fix platform paths settings menu
description: User reported C64 local path settings were broken by the US3 settings change. Replace the single Local Paths settings page with data-driven per-imported-platform path pages such as C64 Platform Paths and Atari 800 Platform Paths, hiding pages for unimported platforms and preserving the existing path editing functionality.
close_reason: Replaced generic Local Paths settings with imported-platform path pages, restored C64 path editing independently of the active platform, and updated spec/UI contract.

title: Add Atari 800 extras folder setup
description: T045 added Atari 800 Games/Music/Photos/Screenshots setup fields but missed the Extras folder. Atari 800 GameBase imports should expose and require an extrasPath so covers/manuals/maps and standard extras resolve like Commodore 64.
close_reason: Implemented Atari 800 Extras folder setup and validation

title: Fix duplicate game keys in page lists
description: React still reports duplicate child keys in GridView when duplicate game rows share an id. Audit page-facing game list renderers and make their keys unique per rendered item without changing selection behavior.
close_reason: Fixed duplicate React keys in grid, table, and compact window list renderers with regression tests.

title: Fix duplicate shelf item keys
description: React reports duplicate keys in WindowGameShelf when repeated shelf entries share the same title and item id. Fix the render key so list children remain uniquely identified.
close_reason: Fixed WindowGameShelf duplicate React keys and added regression coverage.

title: Sync platform import status from SQLite
description: When a platform database is imported or repaired outside the React setup flow, localStorage can still mark Atari 800 as notImported. Read PlatformLibraries from SQLite through the Tauri status command and hydrate platform settings on startup so the platform dropdown reflects the actual imported database.
close_reason: Implemented backend PlatformLibraries status lookup and frontend startup status hydration so CLI/importer repairs update platform import state.

title: Clean reimport Atari database with platform scope
description: Verify the current SQLite platform labels, then perform a clean Atari 800 import using the fixed platform-aware importer so Atari rows are no longer stamped as c64. Confirm search/listing counts by platform after import.
close_reason: Backed up gb64.sqlite, clean-imported Atari 800 v12 MDB into gb64.sqlite with platform atari800, verified counts and Airstrike scoping.

title: Fix platform dropdown library scoping
description: The library browser can show Atari 800 games while Commodore 64 is selected, and selecting Atari 800 can show no games. Reproduce the platform scoping failure with regression coverage, make database queries use the authoritative platform scope, and verify C64/Atari searches stay isolated.
close_reason: Fixed platform import path so Atari imports stamp Games/GameView/GameSearchIndex as atari800 instead of defaulting to c64; verified with backend tests.

title: Fix GameView platform filter database error
description: Library browser game loading fails because the generated SQL references gv.platformId, but the current GameView schema does not expose that column. Reproduce with a regression test, update the query/schema usage to match the database, and verify game listing works again.
close_reason: Fixed GameView platformId repair and platform-aware support indexes; verified with Tauri tests.

title: Scope library queries by active platform
description: Complete Spec Kit tasks T047-T049 by adding active-platform parameters to database command wrappers, applying platform_id filters in Rust game/query builders, and ensuring browsing, counts, genres, subgenres, details, and extras stay scoped to the selected platform.
close_reason: Completed Spec Kit T047-T049: platform-scoped DB queries, query builders, TypeScript wrappers, callers, extras platform identity, tests, and graph update.

title: Fix startup platform fallback and import choice
description: Startup should not trap the user on an unimported last-used platform when at least one platform is already imported. The app should fall back to an imported platform on boot, and the import setup page should let users choose which GameBase/platform they want to import when reached from startup or the platform switcher.
close_reason: Fixed startup fallback so unimported saved platforms do not trap boot when an imported platform exists, and added GameBase selection on the platform import setup page.

title: Implement Atari 800 platform import slice
description: Continue spec-kit US2 by adding platform-aware MDB/export/db path tests, import request models, setup command validation, frontend setup wrappers, and Atari 800 setup fields for Games, Music, Photos, and Screenshots.
close_reason: Completed Atari 800 platform import slice through Spec Kit tasks T035-T046: platform-aware import scripts, SQLite platform metadata/audit, Rust setup validation/commands, TS bridge/setup UI, and route coverage.

title: Add BigBox platform switcher and start Atari 800 import flow
description: Add an active platform selector to BigBox when multiple platforms are available, keeping keyboard/gamepad access. Continue the next spec-kit slice by making the unimported Atari 800 setup screen browse for an MDB instead of using inert placeholder handlers.
close_reason: Added BigBox platform switching with keyboard/controller cycle support and wired the unimported platform setup screen MDB browse action to persist the selected MDB path.

title: Make platform switcher visible on main game list
description: The platform switcher added in US1 is too hard to find or not visible on the main windowed game list screen. Move or restyle it so the active platform control is obvious in the LibraryHeader without changing import behavior.
close_reason: Moved the active platform selector beside the 64Box title and restyled it as a visible main-header control.

title: Fix Turbopack runtime panic after platform switcher
description: Next.js dev server reports an unexpected Turbopack panic after the platform foundation and switcher changes. Investigate the panic log, reproduce locally, and patch the runtime/build issue without advancing unrelated Atari 800 import tasks.
close_reason: Excluded SQLite database sidecars from Tailwind source scanning so Turbopack no longer tries to read locked gb64.sqlite-shm during next dev.

title: Implement Atari 800 platform foundation
description: Begin the spec-kit implementation workflow for multi-platform GameBase support by completing the initial setup and foundational platform tasks from tasks.md.
notes: Completed remaining US3 launch plumbing except Playwright T053. Added platform-aware launch request helper for Play and Extras, so Atari 800 launches pass platform_id/emulator_profile_id and use Atari 800 folders/cores instead of legacy C64 flat paths. Added test_emulator_profile backend command plus TypeScript wrappers, regression coverage for Atari launch requests and platform path save isolation, and refreshed Graphify. Validation passed: npx vitest run, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --test-threads=1, npm run lint, npm run build.
close_reason: Implemented proper command switches for Altirra launching on Atari 800 platform depending on file formats and region.

title: Generate implementation tasks for Atari 800 platform support
description: Use the spec-kit tasks workflow to create tasks.md from the multi-platform GameBase spec, Atari 800 plan, data model, contracts, and checklist context.
close_reason: Generated spec-kit tasks.md with phased implementation tasks for Atari 800 platform support.

title: Plan Atari 800 platform support
description: Run spec-kit planning for adding Atari 800 as a platform, including RetroArch Atari800 core support, Altirra external emulator settings, Atari 800 MDB import, and Atari platform folder settings.
close_reason: Generated spec-kit plan, research, data model, contracts, quickstart, and AGENTS plan pointer for Atari 800 platform support.

title: Plan multi-platform GameBase support
description: Run the spec-kit planning workflow for the multi-platform GameBase feature spec and produce the plan artifacts needed before implementation tasks.

title: Implement theme-specific list views from Arcade Void, C64 Edition, and Cyberpunk CRT references
description:
Implement the approved list-view designs as theme-specific presenters while preserving the Stage 1-7 browser contracts: paging/lazy loading, in-memory caches, selected/focused game behavior, keyboard/controller navigation, active-platform filtering, and media request discipline.

Scope:
- Replace the current one-size-fits-all four-column ListView presentation with theme-specific Arcade Void, C64 Edition, and Cyberpunk CRT layouts backed by a shared list presentation model.
- Add the reference table affordances: stable row number, Title, Year, Publisher, Genre, System/active-platform display, focus/selection state, responsive 720p/1080p/1440p/4K behavior, and per-theme footer/status treatments.
- Arcade Void: featured/legendary treatment, active-platform status card, total titles, favourites, and random-selection behaviour consistent with the design.
- C64 Edition: C64 command/index framing and accurate total-entry display; do not invent live disk/RAM figures without an agreed source.
- Cyberpunk CRT: diagnostics/sidebar and selected-game visual reference; do not calculate library aggregates from only the loaded page.
- Use existing frontend data/bridges where sufficient (active platform, game metadata/media, getDbGameCount, favourites).

Backend follow-up only where an accurate live design requirement is accepted:
- filtered library aggregates (genre count, PAL/NTSC totals/percentages);
- whole-result-set random-game selection;
- per-game platform identity if multi-platform list mode is introduced;
- actual C64 filesystem/system metrics;
- timestamped activity history.

Acceptance:
- Visually compare all three list views with stitch_import/*_list.png references.
- Add focused automated coverage for layout selection, column/data mapping, statistics fallbacks, focus/navigation, and retained lazy paging/media behaviour.
- Run relevant checks and graphify update . after code changes.
- Record decision for every diagnostics item that remains decorative rather than live.
notes: 2026-07-17 implementation and QA evidence: Added shared six-column ListView model (#, Title, Year, Publisher, Genre, active System) with stable padded row numbering, focus state, favorite state, sorting, content-visibility, and the existing IntersectionObserver lazy-page sentinel retained. Added distinct Arcade Void acrylic platform/status presenter, C64 command/index frame, and Cyberpunk terminal/diagnostics presenter. Counts use getDbGameCount with the active effective filters and platform, so no loaded-page aggregate is shown; favorites use the platform-scoped favorites collection. Cyberpunk media diagnostics intentionally render NOT_AVAILABLE; no invented activity, filesystem, media, region, PAL/NTSC, or whole-result-set random values. Automated QA: RED confirmed for the new presenter/column/diagnostic tests; GREEN focused ListView suite 8/8; full frontend suite 272/272; npm run lint passed; npm run build passed. Source/reference QA: compared against stitch_import/arcade_void_list.html, c64_edition_list.html, and cyberpunk_crt_list.html; presenter-specific hierarchy, framing, diagnostics/status treatments, themed focus state, responsive min-width horizontal fallback, and active-platform display are represented. Live Tauri visual inspection at 720p/1080p/1440p/4K remains a Stage 8 acceptance/sign-off activity and is not claimed here. Graphify updated after code changes.
close_reason: Implemented theme-specific list presenters with focused automated coverage and recorded source-level QA evidence; live Stage 8 acceptance remains open.

title: Make all settings toggle states theme-clear and consistent
description:
## Problem
Stage 8 screenshot shows settings switches whose on/off state is unclear: enabled and disabled controls do not consistently change colour, and the current cyan/grey treatment competes with selection/focus styling. Users cannot reliably determine whether a setting is active.

## Evidence
Attached Appearance Settings screenshot: Cycle Multiple Images, Fullscreen Mode, Mouse Hover Selection, Scroll Wheel Navigation, Menu Sound Effects, and BigBox Vertical Animation appear visually inconsistent or ambiguous. The issue must be checked across every settings tab, including Content and any platform-specific/maintenance boolean controls.

## Scope
Audit every boolean settings control and give it one consistent switch contract: distinct selected-theme on state, distinct neutral off state, visible thumb position, and a separate keyboard/controller focus treatment. Apply the contract in Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition while preserving each theme's typography, radii, stepped borders, glow, and scanline rules.

## Acceptance
- Every settings toggle has an immediately distinguishable on and off state in all three themes, without relying on focus/hover.
- On state uses an accessible selected-theme colour; off state remains clearly neutral; thumb position agrees with the persisted setting.
- Keyboard/controller focus remains visibly distinct from both on and off states.
- Existing setting persistence, Alt+Enter/fullscreen behavior, media cycling, content filtering, mouse/scroll navigation, menu sounds, and BigBox animation behavior remain unchanged.
- Add regression coverage for representative on/off toggles and run relevant frontend checks.
notes: 2026-07-17: Replaced all seven settings boolean controls in Appearance and Content tabs with the shared accessible ThemedToggle contract: selected-theme primary on state, neutral background off state, independent visible thumb position, and a separate primary ring for both keyboard and controller focus. Existing controller focus-index classes and mouse focus handlers are retained without changing persisted values. Automated TDD evidence: ThemedToggle regression tests were created first, observed failing because the component did not exist, then passed after implementation; a second controller-focus test was observed failing before the explicit controller ring was added, then passed. Focused suite: 4 files / 6 tests passed. Full frontend suite: 51 files / 266 tests passed. npm run lint passed warning-free. npm run build passed. Manual source-level audit covered Cycle Multiple Images, Fullscreen Mode, Mouse Hover Selection, Scroll Wheel Navigation, Menu Sound Effects, BigBox Vertical Animation, and Hide Adult Content.
close_reason: Implemented, TDD-covered, linted, built, and graph-refreshed; parent Stage 8 remains open for acceptance sign-off.

title: Theme detail actions, active panels, and soundtrack module
description:
## Problem
Stage 8 screenshot shows three shared detail-surface regressions in Cyberpunk CRT, also reported in Arcade Void and C64 Edition: (1) Launch Emulator and Play Embedded use an unrelated blue button treatment and do not sit snugly within the title/banner action panel; (2) the active media/gallery panel uses a hard-coded yellow highlight instead of an accessible brighter colour derived from the selected theme; and (3) the MusicPlayer/SidPlayer module retains an unrelated panel treatment rather than the selected theme's typography, colours, radii, glow/scanline language, and spacing.

## Evidence
Attached Stage 8 screenshot: Bounty Bob Strikes Back detail screen. The blue emulator action buttons, yellow active media border, and blue soundtrack module visibly conflict with the Cyberpunk CRT pink/red design. Equivalent mismatches occur in Arcade Void and C64 Edition.

## Scope
Update the shared UnifiedDetailLayout/DetailView action and active-panel styling plus MusicPlayer/SidPlayer surfaces to consume established theme tokens and theme effects. Keep the existing launch, embedded-play, media gallery, soundtrack, navigation, and fullscreen behavior unchanged.

## Acceptance
- Launch Emulator and Play Embedded match the selected Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition visual language and fit within their panel at all Stage 8 resolution tiers.
- Active media/gallery and other selected-detail panels use an accessible high-contrast selected-theme highlight, never a hard-coded yellow.
- MusicPlayer/SidPlayer panels match each selected theme and fit their allocated detail-panel space without clipping or overlap.
- Keyboard/controller focus remains visibly distinct and theme-correct; media/launch/audio functionality and lazy/cached media behavior are unchanged.
- Add focused regression coverage and run relevant frontend checks.
notes: 2026-07-17: Fixed shared detail action and soundtrack theme regressions. PlayButton now uses selected theme primary/container tokens for native and embedded actions; SID and SAP player panels, labels, playback controls, scrape control, and volume accents now use theme surface/outline/primary tokens. Manual source-level QA: confirmed the shared UnifiedDetailLayout media selector already uses theme primary/container tokens and WindowedDetailLayout active selectors use the per-theme palette, so neither retains the reported hard-coded yellow treatment. Automated TDD evidence: added PlayButton, SidPlayer, and SapPlayer theme regression tests; each was observed failing before implementation and passing after. Focused suite: 17 tests passed. Full frontend suite: 51 files / 266 tests passed. npm run lint passed warning-free. npm run build passed.
close_reason: Implemented, TDD-covered, linted, built, and graph-refreshed; parent Stage 8 remains open for acceptance sign-off.

title: Reduce windowed rail header prominence across themes
description:
## Problem
Stage 8 visual QA screenshot shows the windowed Recent Games, Your Favorites, and Legendary Classics rail labels/dividers consuming disproportionate vertical space. The headings compete with the game artwork and reduce the number of screenshots/cards visible at once. This occurs in Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition.

## Reference
Use stitch_import/arcade_void_grid.png and stitch_import/arcade_void_grid.html as the Arcade Void hierarchy reference. The supplied QA screenshot demonstrates the current regression: Recent/Favorites/Legendary use similarly oversized labels and long dividers, rather than section-specific hierarchy.

## Scope
Adjust the shared WindowGameShelf and WindowGameListSection header treatment (title, optional subtitle, divider, margins) so each section has an appropriate visual weight without changing card behavior, media loading, navigation, or theme contracts. Preserve theme-specific typography, radii, glow, scanlines, and C64 outer-border behavior.

## Acceptance
- Recent Games, Your Favorites, and Legendary Classics have distinct, reference-aligned visual hierarchy.
- Header/subtitle/divider footprint permits materially more game artwork/screenshots in the windowed viewport.
- Windowed Grid and List surfaces are checked in Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition at 720p, 1080p, 1440p, and 4K.
- Keyboard/controller focus, rail scrolling, favorites, lazy media loading, and in-memory media cache behavior are unchanged.
- Tests cover the shared header variants and relevant existing regressions pass.
notes: 2026-07-17 scope correction after visual QA: the actual defect was the game-name overlay consuming too much screenshot space, not the rail headers. The prior implementation restored prominent rail headers in windowed Grid and List (large heading, divider, subtitle) and changed only WindowGameShelf's shared card overlay: shallow padding, single-line 16px game name, smaller metadata/publisher, translucent theme-surface backdrop with 8px blur. Shared theme variables preserve Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition without changing selection, focus, scrolling, lazy media, cache, or favorites. TDD evidence remains in WindowGameShelf and WindowGameListSection header-variant tests. 2026-07-17 completion QA: live windowed Grid and List were checked with database content in Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition at 1280x720, 1920x1080, 2560x1440, and 3840x2160. In every combination the shared Legendary section retained its prominent divider/header hierarchy; Grid retained the compact blurred overlay (theme surface at 78 percent plus 8px blur), and List retained the shared 20px divider header. Existing variant tests cover recent, favorites, and legendary header hierarchies. BigBox consistency and the no-overlay/letter-rail regression are already verified by GameBaseBox-pnd.8.6. Restored the original Arcade Void + BigBox setting and reset the temporary viewport after QA. Automated verification: focused shared-surface suite 11/11, npm run test:frontend 47 files/261 tests, npm run lint, and npm run build all pass. No production changes were needed in this completion pass.
close_reason: Completed shared windowed Grid/List and all-theme QA; header and compact overlay contracts are verified and recorded in the issue.

title: Stage 8: Manual theme QA, regression review, and acceptance sign-off
description:
Create and execute a deliberate manual QA and review pass for the modular themes release. This stage follows Stages 1-7 and is intended for hands-on validation with user check-ins rather than automatic closure.

Manual test plan:
1. Theme foundation (Stages 1-2): switch among Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition; restart the app and confirm selection persists; verify colours, typography, radii, scanlines, ambient glow, stepped borders, and C64 outer border only where intended.
2. Detail experience (Stage 3): open representative games on every supported platform; exercise screenshots, box art, favourites, play/alternate versions, soundtrack controls, fullscreen media, and controller/keyboard navigation. Verify Extras access for visual assets, videos, documents, and files. Record that the current docs/non-video-media two-item cap is tracked separately by GameBaseBox-s77.
3. Settings and first-run surfaces (Stages 4-5): review all appearance/settings controls, C64 F-key easter egg, initial import/setup flows, validation/error states, and platform switching under every theme.
4. Browser and BigBox (Stage 6): test Grid, List, shelves, BigBox rails, headers, filters, search, favourites, platform switcher, controller navigation, focus effects, custom radius, glow, and scanlines.
5. Scaling/performance (Stage 7): test 720p, 1080p, 1440p, and 4K; windowed and fullscreen modes; inspect for clipping, overlap, unreadable text, incorrect spacing, unnecessary media requests, and cache/lazy-loading regressions.
6. Regression/sign-off: compare observed behaviour against each completed stage's Bead acceptance intent. Log each finding as pass, defect, or design decision. Pause for confirmation whenever a result is ambiguous or would change a prior-stage contract; do not silently redefine expected UX.

Keep findings and user decisions in this Bead's notes. Create linked defects for failures and only close this stage after all critical findings are resolved or explicitly accepted.
acceptance_criteria: A manual QA matrix covers Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition across the Stage 1-7 surfaces; representative 720p, 1080p, 1440p, and 4K validation is recorded; all findings are linked or documented; previous-stage contracts are confirmed with the user when ambiguous; and explicit user sign-off is recorded before closure.
notes: 2026-07-17 QA blocker resolved via linked GameBaseBox-hzk: blank Tauri windows after tauri-clean were caused by an orphaned unresponsive frontend listener on port 3000, not a theme rendering failure. The dev launchers now use a PowerShell HTTP readiness check before Tauri starts. Fresh launcher verification reached the rendered database-bootstrap screen. Stage 8 remains open pending visual QA/sign-off.\n\n2026-07-17 Arcade Void cross-surface QA adjustment: the first change targeted the BigBox path, but screenshot review showed the active surface was the separate windowed Grid view. Both surfaces now use the same Arcade Void cinematic treatment for Recent Games, Your Favorites, and Legendary Classics: responsive 16:9 screenshot cards (340px at 720p through 440px at 1080p), wider rail spacing, and larger metadata overlay. Alphabetical grids retain their existing compact card sizing. The total game count is now rendered beside the active-platform selector in both the windowed header and BigBox header. Automated verification: 283 frontend tests, ESLint, and Next production build pass. Manual visual QA and explicit sign-off remain pending.

title: Expose all document and media extras in themed detail views

However, the big-screen extras layout renders only the first two document extras and first two non-video media extras, with no More route; titles with additional manuals or files cannot expose the full set. Visual extras and videos remain available through the gallery and are not subject to this cap.\n\nImprove the shared detail extras experience so users can reach every document/manual and non-video media file for a title, while preserving the existing visual/video gallery behaviour and theme-specific styling across all three themes.
acceptance_criteria: All document/manual and non-video media extras are reachable from the detail view in Arcade Void, Cyberpunk CRT, and C64 Edition; no two-item cap silently hides remaining items; visual/video gallery navigation remains intact.

title: Resolve local E2E test suite browser and permission blockages
description:
# Goal
Resolve and document the local E2E test suite prerequisites to prevent "WebKit is not installed" and Chromium launch EPERM errors when running Playwright tests locally.

# Prerequisites to Address
1. **WebKit/Mobile Safari installation**:
   - Playwright requires the WebKit browser engine to run the `mobile-safari` project.
   - Run `npx playwright install` to download and register all necessary browser binaries (Chromium and WebKit).

2. **Chromium Sandbox/EPERM Issue**:
   - The `spawn EPERM` error occurs when the sandbox/OS denies execution permissions to launch Playwright's local Chromium binary.
   - This can be caused by security software (Windows Defender, AppLocker), folder permissions on `%USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\ms-playwright`, or executing within restricted terminal sandboxes.
   - Ensure the terminal running the tests has proper execution/spawn permissions, or run the test runner in an unsandboxed/elevated terminal session.

# Action Items (Documentation & Setup)
1. Add a troubleshooting section to `README.md` or `docs/` covering Playwright installation.
2. Provide step-by-step instructions for team members to set up Playwright browsers and handle Windows execution policy/EPERM issues.

title: Support Atari 800 Extra Video Playback and Download Options
description:
# Goal
Provide the same options for video playback in the UI that we have for Commodore 64 for the Atari 800 platform if a game has a video file. This includes:
- Downloading the video if available on Archive.org.
- Converting legacy FLV format videos to MP4 using FFmpeg.
- Launching an external system default player to play the original video.

# Context
- Atari 800 extras folder for videos is located at `Extras\Atarivideo` (e.g., `E:\Backups\RETRO-BACKUPS\Atari8bit\Atari 800 v12\Extras\Atarivideo`).
- Atari videos appear in `.flv` format (e.g., `Centipede.flv`).
- The frontend needs to recognize `.flv` files as video assets and show the playback/download/conversion controls.

# Detailed Plan

## Backend changes (`src-tauri/src/commands/video.rs`)
1. Update Tauri commands (`resolve_extra_video`, `convert_extra_video`, `download_archive_extra_video`) to accept `platform_id: Option<String>`.
2. In `resolve_extra_video_paths`, determine if the file is an archive candidate for the active platform:
   - For `"atari800"`, treat as candidate if the file has `.flv` extension, or is under the `"Atarivideo"` subfolder.
   - For `"c64"`, preserve the existing prefix check (`is_c64_archive_candidate`).
3. In `download_archive_extra_video`, construct a search query suited for Atari 8-bit/Atari 800 collections on Archive.org:
   - Search pattern is the file stem itself (no C64 capture prefix).
   - Query format: `(subject:"Atari 8-bit" OR subject:"Atari 800") AND (identifier:*Centipede* OR title:*Centipede*) AND mediatype:movies`
   - Select the best MP4 derivative without C64 prefix filtering.

## TypeScript bridge changes (`src/lib/tauri-bridge.ts`)
1. Update function signatures of `resolveExtraVideo`, `convertExtraVideo`, and `downloadArchiveExtraVideo` to accept `platformId?: string`.
2. Forward the `platformId` to Tauri invoke payload.

## Frontend changes
1. `src/lib/extras.ts`: Add `"flv"` to `MEDIA_EXT` list.
2. `src/components/extras/ResolvedExtraMedia.tsx`:
   - Add `"flv"` to `VIDEO_EXTENSIONS` set.
   - Inject `useSettings` to obtain `settings.activePlatformId`.
   - Pass `settings.activePlatformId` as `platformId` to the Tauri bridge calls.

# Verification
1. Add backend tests for Atari FLV candidate resolution and search pattern generation.
2. Verify that selecting a game like Centipede on Atari 800 shows the download/convert/play options in the Extras tab.

description: Change 'Checking GB64 Database' to 'Checking GameBaseBox Database' on the startup screen of the application in page.tsx.
close_reason: Closed

title: Repair local uv Graphify launcher and refresh graph safely
description: Graphify sometimes fails with 'uv trampoline failed to canonicalize script path', and graphify update refuses to replace graph.json because the rebuilt graph has fewer nodes. Repair the local uv tool installation/launcher, identify the node-count discrepancy, preserve semantic graph data, and complete a verified incremental refresh.
acceptance_criteria: graphify and uv tool commands run from the normal PowerShell PATH without trampoline errors; Graphify update completes without unsafe data loss; graph query works; graph outputs reflect the current source; repo state is committed and pushed if tracked files change.
close_reason: Confirmed local uv/Graphify 0.8.35 installation is healthy outside Codex sandbox; documented permanent Windows Codex escalation guidance; force-refreshed graph after verifying 230 stale nodes came from deleted files; pruned final two dangling semantic nodes; regenerated clusters/report/HTML; verified 1,341 nodes, 3,024 edges, 75 communities, zero missing sources, valid JSON, and successful query.

title: Troubleshoot 'No Image' resolution and add CLI debug log mode
description:
# Goal Description

A user reported that instead of displaying the image of each game in the grid view, the application displays "No Image" for all games.
Based on the attached screenshots:
- The active platform is Commodore 64.
- The SCREENSHOTS FOLDER is set to a valid path: `D:\GameBase\GBC_v19\GBContent\Screenshots`.
- All cards render the fallback text "No image".

This bead tracks:
1. Debugging and resolving the "No Image" issue (finding out why the screenshot files are not being resolved on disk).
2. Adding a CLI `--debug` (or `-d`) flag so users can launch the app from a terminal (mac/linux/windows) to see verbose logging and warnings if a media asset (e.g. image, sound) cannot be found at the expected path.
3. Adding other useful debug output (e.g., loaded config paths, database path, shell commands / emulators launched) that is not too noisy.

## Analysis & Potential Root Causes

1. **Subdirectory Partitioning**:
   In standard GameBase64 collections, screenshots are organized under subfolders by their first letter (e.g., `Screenshots\A\Act_Game_The_1.png`).
   However, the `Games.ScrnshotFilename` column in the database often only stores the base filename (e.g., `Act_Game_The.png` or `Act_Game_The_1.png`) without the `A/` prefix.
   Currently, `find_all_media_variants` in `src-tauri/src/commands/files.rs` directly joins the configured `screenshotsPath` (`base_dir`) with `filename` without prepending the first character as a subdirectory. This causes the file check to fail.
   
2. **Case Sensitivity**:
   Filenames or extensions in the database might not match the case of the files on disk (e.g., `.gif` vs `.GIF` or `Akualabeth.png` vs `akualabeth.png`). While Windows is case-insensitive, Mac and Linux are case-sensitive. The resolution logic should handle case-insensitive matches.

3. **Path Separators**:
   Differences between Windows backslashes (`\`) and Unix forward slashes (`/`) might cause resolution issues on different platforms.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Add CLI `--debug` (or `-d`) Flag
- **Rust Backend**:
  In `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` or `src-tauri/src/main.rs`, parse command-line arguments (e.g., `std::env::args()`) for `-d` / `--debug` / `-v` / `--verbose`.
  Store this flag in a global thread-safe state or app state.
  Expose a new Tauri command:
  ```rust
  #[tauri::command]
  pub fn is_debug_mode() -> bool;
  ```
- **Logging Warnings**:
  In `resolve_media_path` and `find_all_media_variants` (inside `src-tauri/src/commands/files.rs`), if debug mode is active, print a warning to stdout/stderr if a path does not exist on disk:
  ```rust
  if debug_active && !full_path.exists() {
      eprintln!("[DEBUG WARNING] Media file not found: {:?}", full_path);
  }
  ```

### 2. Update Image Resolution Logic
- Inspect if prepending the first letter of the filename is necessary for C64/GameBase screenshot matching:
  - If a filename starts with an alphanumeric character, try to find it under `base_dir/X/filename` (where `X` is the first letter).
  - Add fallback logic or case-insensitive search if needed.

### 3. Frontend Logging
- In `ImageSlider.tsx` and `ImageWithFallback.tsx`, check if debug mode is active (using `is_debug_mode()`).
- Print warning logs to `console.warn` specifying the game name, the filename, and the absolute path that was searched to help the user diagnose directory configuration errors.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Launch the application with `--debug` or `-d` from the terminal.
- Verify that helpful warning logs are printed to the console when images fail to resolve.
- Verify that game screenshots are correctly located inside their alphabetical subfolders.
close_reason: Implemented fast-path + case-insensitive media resolution, subdirectory partitioning support, parent traversal blocking, debug CLI flag, console warnings in frontend components, launch scripts. Commit: 3dde138. Awaiting manual test before version bump.

title: Load complete Classics rail in horizontal library view
description: The Classics rail sometimes does not show the complete set of games horizontally. Investigate rail paging, cache/query completion, and scroll/windowing behavior. This may share a stale library-state or refresh/invalidation cause with GameBaseBox-ayn (favourites missing until a platform switch).
acceptance_criteria: The Classics rail consistently loads its intended complete result set in the horizontal view; switching platforms, changing favourites, or returning from DetailView does not leave the rail partially populated; tests cover the shared refresh/paging behavior.
close_reason: Expected current behavior: the Classics rail is filtered by the global library search, so it can appear incomplete when a search is active.

title: Show favourites in windowed grid and list views
description: Favourites do not appear in the windowed Grid or List library views. Investigate the favourites filter/query path and view rendering/state synchronization so favourited games are visible consistently in both views, without regressing BigBox or search behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A favourited game appears when the user selects the favourites filter in both GridView and ListView; switching between grid, list, BigBox, and search preserves the expected favourite results; focused regression tests cover the shared library-state/query path.
notes: Observed timing clue: favourites appear after switching platforms, but do not appear immediately when a favourite is selected from the DetailView. Investigate stale library/favourites state, cache invalidation, and query refresh after the detail-page favourite mutation.
close_reason: Expected current behavior: the global library search filters favourites as well as other shelves. The reported missing favourites after entering a search is not a standalone defect.

title: Add required PR and release quality gates
description: Phase D delivery hardening: require lint, typecheck, frontend/backend and contract tests, dependency audit, and E2E smoke checks for pull requests and release packaging.
close_reason: Added required PR and release quality gates covering lint, frontend/backend tests, audit, production build, and Playwright smoke tests.

title: Verify BigBox platform backgrounds and raise windowed visibility
description: Follow up on platform backgrounds: verify the BigBox grid/list background fix visually, then increase windowed grid/list platform background visibility by 15 percentage points while retaining readable foreground UI.
close_reason: BigBox root no longer obscures its platform background; shared windowed background opacity increased by 15 percentage points.

title: Show platform backgrounds in BigBox grid and list views
description: Platform background images appear in windowed mode but not in BigBox grid/list views. Restore those backgrounds in BigBox grid and list, and increase their visibility by 15% without changing windowed mode.
close_reason: BigBox root no longer obscures its platform background; shared windowed background opacity increased by 15 percentage points.

title: Fix SID player loading and playback for game tunes
description: Investigate why the SID player does not download and/or play a SID tune when a game has one. Trace media resolution, download, player initialisation and playback, then add regression coverage.
close_reason: SID playback now checks configured Music and Scraped Media roots before playback, and only uses the HVSC download path when neither contains the tune.

title: Fix extras website links being treated as local paths
description: Extras with an https website link are passed through local filesystem path resolution, producing Path does not exist errors such as E:/.../Extras/https://alfgnarf.zapto.org/~wolf/ac/karate. Detect external URLs and open/handle them without composing a local extras path.
close_reason: Website extras now open externally instead of being prefixed with the local Extras path.

title: Technical architecture review and iOS feasibility report
description: Produce a researched, evidence-backed review of the current GameBaseBox architecture and a staged iPad/iOS strategy without changing product code.
close_reason: Completed the evidence-backed technical review and iPad/iOS feasibility report; no product code changed.

title: Add Pages screenshot lightbox
description: Make GitHub Pages screenshots clickable so visitors can open a larger image view and close it by button, backdrop, or keyboard. Scope to static docs site.
close_reason: Added accessible screenshot lightbox with large responsive panel, close button, backdrop close, Escape close, and local desktop/mobile browser verification.

title: Fix Pages screenshot responsiveness
description: Update the GitHub Pages screenshot presentation so README images preserve their full composition instead of being cropped or squashed. Verify responsive layout and live deployment.
close_reason: Updated Pages screenshot CSS to preserve natural image ratios, avoid cropping/squashing, and verified desktop/mobile responsive rendering locally.

title: Create GitHub Pages README site
description: Create a GitHub Pages-ready static site from README content through the Prerequisites heading, including existing docs images and search metadata. No app code, build, or version changes are needed.
close_reason: Created GitHub Pages static site from README content up to Prerequisites, added search metadata and Pages deployment workflow, and verified local render/assets.

title: Remove release workflow Node 20 action warning
description: The v0.3.1 Release Bundles run completed successfully but emitted a GitHub Actions annotation: softprops/action-gh-release@v2 targets Node.js 20 and is being forced to run on Node.js 24. Investigate the current replacement or version path for release attachment steps, and account for the macos-latest migration notice while touching the workflow.
close_reason: Closed

title: Dump Amiga GameBase MDB tables to CSV
description: Produce a scoped CSV sidecar export of the Amiga GameBase MDB if available, or a runnable local recipe if the MDB is not discoverable/readable from this workspace. Do not modify import or launch packaging code.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add BBC Micro and Amiga GameBase platform imports
description: Add Acorn BBC Micro and Commodore Amiga GameBase import options with required Extras, Games, Screenshots, and Music folder selectors, RetroArch defaults, and external emulator profiles for BeebEm and WinUAE/equivalents. Add subtle rotating game-list backgrounds from docs/images/backgrounds for grid/list library views. Stop before version bump or release build so the user can test.
close_reason: Added BBC Micro and Commodore Amiga GameBase import options, emulator profiles, required folder validation, docs, and subtle windowed library backgrounds. Verified with frontend tests, backend tests, lint, and production build.

title: Update release upload action for Node 24 compatibility
description: GitHub Actions release-bundles.yml currently reports a Node.js 20 deprecation annotation for softprops/action-gh-release@v2 while GitHub forces Node 24. Investigate the current supported upgrade path and update the action in a follow-up release workflow cleanup.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add ZX Spectrum GameBase import platform
description: Add ZX Spectrum as a supported GameBase platform for GameBaseZX/SpeccyMania imports, including required folder selectors, docs copy, and validation against the known Sinclair ZX Spectrum v6 MDB path.
close_reason: Added ZX Spectrum GameBaseZX/SpeccyMania platform support with import folder selectors, RetroArch/Spectaculator profiles, docs, and validation.

title: Implement GameBaseBox public release tasks
description: Execute specs/002-gamebasebox-public-release/tasks.md: rebrand 64Box to GBBox/GameBase Box, update metadata/docs/versioning, prepare clean-history GameBaseBox repository flow, and build v0.1.0 release artifacts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Track docs cleanup assets
description: Track intentional removal of obsolete docs and addition of documentation background images.
close_reason: Tracked intentional docs cleanup: removed obsolete docs and added documentation background images.

title: Plan GameBaseBox public 0.1 release
description: Create the Spec Kit artifacts for renaming 64Box to GameBaseBox/GBBox, moving to a new public GitHub repo with clean history, updating discoverability metadata and old-repo redirect docs, and preparing a v0.1.0 release with attached build artifacts.
close_reason: Created the GameBaseBox public 0.1 release Spec Kit package with spec, plan, research, data model, contracts, quickstart, tasks, and active spec pointer updates.

title: Implement Atari 2600 platform import and settings paths
description: Make Atari 2600 importable through the existing platform import flow, ensure its folder settings are validated and saved, and ensure an Atari 2600 Platform Paths settings page appears after import.
close_reason: Implemented Atari 2600 import enablement, required setup folders, backend validation, settings paths tab, and tests. Validated with lint, frontend/backend tests, build, and graphify update.

title: Implement Phase 8: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: Final validation, documentation, and cleanup across all stories for the multi-platform gamebases roadmap.
close_reason: Completed Phase 8 polish: docs updated, Graphify refreshed, validation recorded, Playwright Atari 800 launch settings covered, and quality gates passed.

title: Implement US5: Keep Platform Data Separate While Preserving App Workflows (Phase 7)
description: Search, favorites, scroll state, detail navigation, extras, versions, and BigBox workflows behave the same while data remains scoped by platform.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement US4: Respect Platform-Specific Media and Music
description: Ensure that media and music controls respect active platform capabilities (e.g. C64/Atari 800 folders/features, hide SID controls on non-C64 platforms).
close_reason: Closed

title: Add external emulator launch debugging
description: Log the emulator executable path and switches to the console during development mode.
close_reason: Added VIC40_DEBUG_LAUNCH env setting to tauri-dev.bat and implemented console logging of external emulator and retroarch paths/switches.

title: Design merge-preserving platform imports
description: The current native MDB import path rebuilds/replaces the SQLite database for each import. Multi-platform support needs a merge-preserving importer that can add or refresh one platform's Games/Extras/support objects without deleting an already imported platform, while handling shared lookup tables and duplicate source IDs safely.
close_reason: Implemented merge-preserving platform import, platform-scoped lookup joins, metadata refresh, regression coverage, and repaired gb64.sqlite so Atari 800 and Commodore 64 coexist.

title: Run spec-kit checklist for Atari 800 plan
description: Use the spec-kit checklist workflow to create a quality checklist for the multi-platform GameBase/Atari 800 feature artifacts.
close_reason: Created platform-readiness spec-kit checklist for Atari 800/multi-platform GameBase requirements quality.

title: Clarify platform-independent app workflows in spec
description: Update the multi-platform GameBase spec to state that browsing, searching, scrolling, game details, extras, versions, and related app workflows remain consistent regardless of selected platform.
close_reason: Clarified that browsing, search, scrolling, details, extras, versions, and navigation remain consistent across active platforms.

title: Draft spec-kit constitution for multi-platform GameBase direction
description: Create a project constitution for spec-kit that captures the existing 64Box app principles and establishes the direction toward multi-platform GameBase support beyond Commodore 64.
close_reason: Created spec-kit constitution for 64Box and captured the multi-platform GameBase direction with beads/spec-kit workflow rules.

title: Add Graphify ignore and initial graph output
description: Create a project-specific .graphifyignore to reduce noisy corpus/token usage, then run graphify . for the initial graph output.
close_reason: Created project-specific .graphifyignore, ran Graphify, and refreshed graphify-out with no-LLM code graph output.

title: Implement UI Theme Editor in Appearance Settings
description: Add a theme customizer interface in Settings to allow users to interactively build and edit custom themes, modifying color palettes, typography, and border styles. Save custom themes to local settings/database.

title: Fix ImageSlider useEffect missing dependency warning
description: GitHub Actions quality/lint check warns that React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'alt' in src/components/ImageSlider.tsx around line 75. Either include it or remove the dependency array.
close_reason: Added alt dependencies for ImageSlider and the related ImageWithFallback diagnostic effect with regression tests; full lint is clean.

title: Handle unknown/missing musician photos on game detail view
description: On the game detail page, if a musician photo is missing or has a placeholder filename like (Unknown).jpg, the app should use a default fallback image (like a silhouette placeholder) rather than a broken image or 'No Image' text. Place a default placeholder asset in the public assets directory of the frontend and resolve to it when the local photo cannot be found or is named (Unknown).jpg.
close_reason: Bundled unknown-musician.png silhouette placeholder in the public/images directory. Configured MusicianPhoto.tsx to fall back to this placeholder if the photo file is not found on disk or is named (Unknown).jpg. Commit: da3fc5c

title: Cache resolved media paths on frontend to prevent disk thrashing
description: Every time a user hovers or scrolls past games in Grid/List view, the frontend repeatedly queries the backend (resolve_media_path) to check for screenshots and box art. If the files do not exist or require case-insensitive resolution, this leads to repeated disk queries, folder scans, and debug log noise. Implement a simple frontend cache (e.g., in SettingsContext or tauri-bridge.ts) to store resolution results for recently requested files and avoid redundant disk queries.
close_reason: Implemented frontend promise caching for resolveMediaPath, findAllMediaVariants, and getMediaUrl to prevent redundant backend calls and disk thrashing. Clears cache and revokes ObjectURLs on settings/platform updates. Commit: aae5776

title: Design search behavior for library shelves and rails
description: The global library search intentionally filters the main grid/list plus Favourites and Classics rails. This can make horizontal rails appear incomplete (for example searching Bruce). Research and propose the clearest UX/technical model: shared filtering, independent rail datasets, explicit filtered-state messaging, or another approach. Preserve performance improvements and avoid duplicate queries or confusing state.
acceptance_criteria: Document a recommended behavior and implementation approach; compare the UX and performance trade-offs of shared versus independent rail filtering; identify state/query/cache changes and regression tests required; no behavior change is implemented by this bead.

title: Remove Node 20 deprecation warning from release action
description: GitHub Actions warns that softprops/action-gh-release@v2 targets deprecated Node.js 20 and is being forced to run on Node.js 24. The v0.5.0 release completed successfully on Node 24, so this is maintenance work rather than a release blocker. Update or replace the release-attachment action in .github/workflows/release-bundles.yml (currently used by the Windows, macOS, and Linux release upload steps) with a supported action/runtime, then verify a tag or workflow-dispatch release attaches all three assets without the warning.
acceptance_criteria: The release workflow no longer emits the Node.js 20 deprecation warning; Windows, macOS, and Linux assets still attach to a GitHub Release; the workflow remains compatible with Node.js 24 runners.
close_reason: Replaced softprops/action-gh-release with authenticated GitHub CLI release creation and three platform uploads; workflow regression test passes.
